items.title: Jira's Latest Update Allows AI Agents and Humans to Work Side by Side

content.abstract: Atlassian enables AI agent task management in Jira alongside human workers, marking a significant step in human-AI workplace collaboration.

items.title: About 12% of US Teens Turn to AI for Emotional Support or Advice

content.abstract: 12% of US teens use AI chatbots for emotional support, raising concerns among mental health professionals about AI's unsuitability for therapeutic guidance.

items.title: Self-Driving Tech Startup Wayve Raises $1.2B from Nvidia, Uber, and Three Automakers

content.abstract: UK autonomous driving company Wayve secures $1.2 billion from Nvidia, Uber and three automakers for its end-to-end AI approach to self-driving.

items.title: Nvidia Challenger AI Chip Startup MatX Raised $500M

content.abstract: AI chip startup MatX, founded by former Google TPU engineers, raises $500M to challenge Nvidia's GPU dominance with specialized AI hardware.

items.title: Stripe Is Reportedly Eyeing Deal to Buy Some or All of PayPal

content.abstract: Payment giant Stripe exploring acquisition of PayPal, potentially creating a dominant digital payments platform and reshaping fintech industry.

items.title: US Tells Diplomats to Lobby Against Foreign Data Sovereignty Laws

content.abstract: Trump administration instructs US diplomats to actively oppose foreign data sovereignty regulations, prioritizing American tech companies' global data access.

items.title: Apple Rolls Out Age Verification Tools Worldwide

content.abstract: Apple deploys global age-verification mechanisms to comply with emerging child safety regulations, setting new industry standards.

items.title: Google's New 1.9GW Clean Energy Deal Includes Massive 100-Hour Battery

content.abstract: Google commits to 1.9GW clean energy deal featuring Form Energy's 100-hour iron-air batteries for data center operations, a breakthrough in long-duration energy storage.

items.title: India AI Boom Pushes Firms to Trade Near-Term Revenue for Users

content.abstract: AI providers including ChatGPT and Gemini prioritize user acquisition over revenue in India, signaling a new phase in global AI market expansion.

items.title: Former L3Harris Trenchant Boss Jailed for Selling Hacking Tools to Russian Broker

content.abstract: Former L3Harris executive sentenced to 7 years for stealing and selling hacking tools to Russian entities, highlighting national security risks in defense tech.

items.title: Spanish Soonicorn Multiverse Computing Releases Free Compressed AI Model

content.abstract: Multiverse Computing releases free HyperNova 60B compressed model claiming superior performance to Mistral, advancing democratized AI access.

items.title: Just Pull a String to Turn Tile Patterns into 3D Structures

content.abstract: MIT algorithm enables flat tile patterns to transform into 3D structures via single-string actuation for deployable medical devices and emergency shelters.

items.title: A New Way to Rejuvenate the Immune System

content.abstract: MIT/Broad Institute researchers program liver cells via mRNA nanoparticles to produce T-cell stimulating signals, helping aged mice respond to vaccines and cancer treatment.

items.title: AI-Designed Proteins May Help Spot Cancer

content.abstract: MIT/Microsoft use AI to design peptides detecting cancer enzymes in circulation, enabling early cancer detection through urine tests.

items.title: A Retinal Reboot for Amblyopia

content.abstract: MIT neuroscientist discovers that temporarily anesthetizing an amblyopic eye's retina restores neural connections, potentially treating lazy eye in adults.

items.title: Hydrofluorocarbon Electrolytes for Energy-Dense and Low-Temperature Batteries

content.abstract: Novel hydrofluorocarbon electrolytes enable lithium-metal batteries with high energy density capable of operating at extreme low temperatures.

items.title: Rewiring an E3 Ligase Enhances Cold Resilience and Phosphate Use in Maize

content.abstract: Genetic engineering of NLA enzyme improves cold tolerance and phosphate uptake in maize with field-tested yield improvements.

items.title: Field-Free Full Switching of Chiral Antiferromagnetic Order

content.abstract: Mn3Sn homo-junction achieves efficient field-free switching of antiferromagnetic materials for energy-efficient magnetic memory devices.

items.title: Human Hippocampal Neurogenesis in Adulthood, Ageing and Alzheimer's Disease

content.abstract: Multiomic single-cell sequencing identifies distinct neurogenesis signatures distinguishing cognitive preservation from decline in aging brains.

items.title: Coral Microbiomes as Reservoirs of Unknown Genomic and Biosynthetic Diversity

content.abstract: Analysis of 820 coral colonies across Pacific reefs reveals vast molecular diversity in microbial communities, emphasizing reef conservation importance.

items.title: Pancreatic-Targeted Lipid Nanoparticles Based on Organ Capsule Filtration

content.abstract: Novel lipid nanoparticles enable precise therapeutic nucleic acid delivery to pancreatic tissue for pancreatic disease treatment.

items.title: OR7A10 GPCR Engineering Boosts CAR-NK Therapy Against Solid Tumours

content.abstract: Gene boosters enhance CAR construct expression for scalable CAR-NK cell therapies targeting solid tumors.

items.title: Cavity-Altered Superconductivity

content.abstract: New platform using hexagonal boron nitride with molecular superconductors achieves cavity-altered materials with enhanced magnetic properties.

items.title: AI Is Acing Math Exams Faster Than Scientists Write Them

content.abstract: AI models solving math benchmarks at accelerating rates, with Frontier Math expected to saturate within two years as AI reasoning advances.

items.title: Blood Pressure Monitoring via Smartwatch Radio Signals

content.abstract: UT Austin demonstrates non-contact blood pressure monitoring using reflected radio signals, avoiding skin-tone bias and enabling smartwatch integration within two years.

items.title: AI Data Centers Turn to High-Temperature Superconductors

content.abstract: Major cloud providers adopting superconductor cables for AI data centers, with Microsoft investing $75M in Veir's rare-earth conductor technology.

items.title: The Age-Verification Trap: Privacy Paradox

content.abstract: Age-verification enforcement creates surveillance infrastructure conflicting with data-protection regulations, presenting a fundamental privacy paradox.

items.title: Danish Government Agency to Ditch Microsoft Software

content.abstract: Denmark's government agency announces exit from Microsoft products, signaling growing European digital independence movement.

items.title: Apple Accelerates US Manufacturing with Mac Mini Production in Houston

content.abstract: Apple opens new Houston facility for Mac mini production, accelerating reshoring of technology manufacturing to the United States.

items.title: Mercury 2: Fast Reasoning LLM Powered by Diffusion

content.abstract: Inception Labs introduces Mercury 2, a fast reasoning LLM using novel diffusion architecture rather than traditional autoregressive approach.

items.title: Moonshine Open-Weights STT Models — Higher Accuracy Than Whisper Large v3

content.abstract: Open-weight speech recognition models surpass OpenAI Whisper Large v3 in accuracy, advancing open-source AI capabilities.

items.title: Attacks on Ukraine's Health Care Increased by 20% in 2025

content.abstract: WHO documents 2,881 healthcare attacks since 2022, with medical warehouse attacks tripling. 72% of Ukrainians experience anxiety/depression.

items.title: Organized Crime and Poor Regulation Drive Toxic Pollution Threat

content.abstract: UN experts warn that illegal waste trafficking could surge due to weak regulation and criminal networks across continents.

items.title: Ukraine: Diplomatic Tools Essential as Invasion Enters Fifth Year

content.abstract: Senior UN official urges Security Council to use every diplomatic tool as Russia's invasion of Ukraine completes four years.

items.title: Somalia's Hunger Crisis Doubles to 6.5 Million

content.abstract: Acute food insecurity in Somalia doubles year-over-year to 6.5 million due to drought, conflict, and rising food prices.

items.title: Ukraine Reconstruction Costs $588 Billion Over Decade

content.abstract: Reconstruction needs nearly triple Ukraine's GDP as housing, energy, and infrastructure face continuous attacks, totaling $588 billion.

items.title: Brookings Experts on the Supreme Court's Tariff Decision

content.abstract: Multi-expert analysis of Supreme Court ruling on tariff authority with implications for trade policy and executive power.

items.title: Food Inflation Strikes, El Nino Looms, Biodiversity Talks Stagnate

content.abstract: Mediterranean storms threaten European food prices (Spain loses 20% production). 60% chance La Nina shifting to El Nino by spring, potentially making 2026-2027 warmest on record.

items.title: NSF Director Nomination: Jim O'Neill from Thiel Foundation

content.abstract: Trump administration plans to nominate Jim O'Neill (former HHS, Thiel Foundation) as NSF director, emphasizing 'science over ideology'.

items.title: NIST Foreign Researcher Three-Year Limit Policy

content.abstract: NIST implements three-year limit on international graduate students and postdocs, raising concerns about preventing foreign doctoral completion.

items.title: NIST Launches AI Agent Standards Initiative

content.abstract: NIST launches AI Agent Standards Initiative with NSF to develop industry-led AI agent protocols and standards.

items.title: Black Americans' Family Network Extends Beyond Blood Ties

content.abstract: Research reveals broader definitions of family among Black Americans with extensive emotional and financial support networks beyond biological relatives.
